metadata.title: Cardbound MUD
metadata.description: A Saturday-morning modern city where trading card monsters burst from every deck and cause spectacularly manageable havoc.

zones.name: Binder Downtown
zones.description: A modern shopping district where living cards sprint between buses, billboards, and snack kiosks.

zones.name: Neon Mall
zones.description: A bright indoor maze of escalators, closed storefronts, and monsters using sale banners as capes.

zones.name: Summon Park
zones.description: A city park and toy hangar where companion monsters, model frames, and ambitious kids all need supervision.

zones.name: Crossover Harbor
zones.description: The waterfront convention grounds where every card genre is trying to have its finale at once.

doors.name: sleeve turnstile
doors.description: A mall turnstile wrapped in glowing protective sleeves.

doors.name: frame bay door
doors.description: A rolling hangar door with a toy-sized launch rail bolted across it.

doors.name: crossover gate
doors.description: A convention-stage gate made of clashing card borders and bad timing.

quests.name: Starter Deck Panic
quests.summary: Open the mall route and make sure the information kiosk survived first contact with living cards.
quests.description: Marshal Echo needs the emergency sleeve key from the card shop. Get the key, open the mall turnstile, reach the concourse, and check in with Milo at the kiosk.

steps.label: Learn from Marshal Echo why the mall gate needs the sleeve key.
steps.objective: Ask Marshal Echo about the starter kit.

steps.label: Take the sleeve key from Sleeve Queen Card Shop.

quests.name: Citywide Signal
quests.summary: Help the food court radio crew broadcast monster warnings across Binder Bay.
quests.description: Signal DJ Pax can warn the city if you gather an antenna sticker, retrieve the rooftop signal booster, and tell Bea to fire up the mast.

steps.label: Take the antenna sticker from the transit platform.
Take the signal booster from the broadcast roof.

quests.name: Park Roundup
quests.summary: Help Ranger Mira calm the small companion monsters before they learn capitalism from the hot dog cart.
quests.description: The park monsters are scared, hungry, and fast. Gather capture tags, check Pocket Park, and report back to Mira.

steps.label: Take a bundle of capture tags.

quests.name: Mana Leak Mayhem
quests.summary: Sort out the pop-up mana lab before the colors start making policy decisions.
quests.description: Professor Amper needs a prism shard and a vault check to stabilize the mall's loose mana.

steps.label: Ask Professor Amper about the prism leak.
Take a prism shard from the mana lab.
Take the prism badge from the vault.

quests.name: Frame Bay Havoc
quests.summary: Get into the snap-frame hangar and stop the model kits from inventing dramatic launch procedures.
quests.description: Mechanic Joss needs the servo key recovered from the training lot. Unlock the frame bay, enter the hangar, and check the launch rail.

steps.label: Ask Mechanic Joss about the frame bay.
Take the servo key from the training lot.
Talk to Mechanic Joss in the hangar.

quests.name: Crossover Calamity
quests.summary: Open the final pavilion and stop every card genre from ending the episode at the same time.
quests.description: Coordinator Vale has the pass, the stage has the gate, and the pavilion has too many finales. Get the pass, open the crossover gate, and face the Genre-Jam Titan.

steps.label: Ask Coordinator Vale about the crossover.
Take the crossover pass from the preview stage.
Tell Captain Rook the finale route is open.

rooms.name: Binder Square
rooms.description: Emergency cones, folding tables, and glowing booster wrappers fill the plaza. A giant sign blinks: KEEP CALM AND SLEEVE RESPONSIBLY.

rooms.name: Sleeve Queen Card Shop
rooms.description: Binders lie open like emergency manuals. A bell over the door rings every time a loose monster card tries to sneak back in.

rooms.name: Sleeve Alley
rooms.description: A narrow alley glittering with penny sleeves, runaway dice, and one suspiciously heroic trash can.

rooms.name: Mall Gate Turnstiles
rooms.description: The mall entrance is blocked by turnstiles wrapped in glowing sleeves. Beyond them, escalators roar like dramatic theme music.

south.blockedMessage: The sleeve turnstile flashes red. It wants the sleeve key from the card shop.

rooms.name: Food Court Barricade
rooms.description: Pretzel carts form a defensive wall. Someone has written DO NOT FEED THE HOLO-DRAGON on a napkin dispenser.

rooms.name: Transit Platform
rooms.description: The buses have stopped, but their route signs keep flipping to boss-monster names. Commuters clap whenever one spells anything useful.

rooms.name: Neon Mall Concourse
rooms.description: Escalators move on their own, ad screens shout combo advice, and a kiosk kid is using receipt paper to track monster sightings.

rooms.name: Arcade of Flashing Buttons
rooms.description: Cabinets beep in panic. A claw machine has captured three tiny monsters and somehow elected itself mayor.

rooms.name: Rooftop Stairs
rooms.description: Emergency lights chase each other up the stairwell while card borders peel off the walls like stickers.

rooms.name: Broadcast Roof
rooms.description: A pirate radio mast has been rebuilt from selfie sticks and foil card dividers. The whole roof hums with heroic static.

rooms.name: Service Hall
rooms.description: A staff-only corridor has become a rulebook-only corridor. The vending machines accept coins, cards, and sincere apologies.

rooms.name: Pop-Up Mana Lab
rooms.description: A science fair booth has grown into a lab of colored lights, safety goggles, and equations ending in several exclamation marks.

rooms.name: Prism Vault
rooms.description: A former storage room now contains a floating prism that sorts loose magic into colors, then immediately argues with itself.

rooms.name: Toy Aisle Checkpoint
rooms.description: Action figures stand in perfect formation beside half-empty shelves. Someone has taped googly eyes over the security camera.

rooms.name: Crossover Preview Stage
rooms.description: A small convention stage is covered in mismatched banners. Every microphone introduces a different genre.

south.blockedMessage: The crossover gate needs a pass stamped by every genre station.

rooms.name: Pocket Park
rooms.description: A duck pond has been covered with floating card sleeves. Tiny monsters chase frisbees while volunteers pretend this is manageable.

rooms.name: Park Ranger Card Table
rooms.description: A ranger table is covered with clipboards, water bottles, and a hand-drawn chart titled THINGS THAT ARE CUTE BUT STILL BITE.

rooms.name: Training Lot
rooms.description: Parking spaces have been repainted as battle lanes. Cones, chalk arrows, and one very stern whistle keep the chaos almost organized.

west.blockedMessage: The frame bay door needs the servo key from the training lot.

rooms.name: Snap-Frame Hangar
rooms.description: Model frames hang from fishing line, half heroic and half unfinished. A launch rail points directly at several warning signs.

rooms.name: Harbor Walk
rooms.description: Food trucks, convention booths, and emergency tape line the waterfront. The bay reflects monsters that are not actually above it yet.

rooms.name: Captain Pier
rooms.description: A pirate-themed booth has become a leadership seminar with barrels, banners, and suspiciously stretchy hazards.

rooms.name: Crossover Pavilion
rooms.description: The final pavilion is a storm of card frames, toy parts, snack wrappers, and heroic speeches arriving out of order.

items.name: sleeve key
items.description: A laminated keycard that smells faintly of fresh booster packs.

items.name: energy drink
items.description: A neon can promising focus, sparkle, and only mild regret.

items.name: foil snack
items.description: A snack cake wrapped in shiny foil. Somehow already tournament legal.

items.name: antenna sticker
items.description: A lightning-bolt sticker that boosts any signal and most egos.

items.name: signal booster
items.description: A taped-together booster made from selfie sticks, foil rares, and optimism.

items.name: capture tags
items.description: Soft plastic tags for marking panicked monsters as rescued instead of rampaging.

items.name: prism shard
items.description: A rainbow-edged shard that hums in five different arguments.

items.name: prism badge
items.description: A safety badge proving you survived the lab without becoming a color wheel.

items.name: servo key
items.description: A tiny motor key with a tag reading DO NOT LAUNCH INDOORS.

items.name: captain token
items.description: A plastic coin stamped with a smiling skull and a very unofficial motto.

items.name: crossover pass
items.description: A convention badge stamped by multiple genre stations and one confused pretzel vendor.

items.name: finale binder
items.description: A reinforced binder with dividers labeled Plans A through Saturday.

items.name: duel disk holster
items.description: A hip holster for quick dramatic card reveals.

items.name: trainer jacket
items.description: A bright jacket with snack pockets, tag loops, and bite-resistant sleeves.

items.name: lucky cap
items.description: A cap turned exactly the right amount backward for confidence.

items.name: street sneakers
items.description: Scuffed sneakers with excellent grip on mall tile and questionable reality.

items.name: mana pin
items.description: A little enamel pin that changes color whenever rules are nearby.

items.name: pilot gloves
items.description: Fingerless gloves for pilots, builders, and people about to press the big red button.

items.name: guest star sticker
items.description: A holographic sticker that looks like it belongs to six different shows.

items.name: promo foil stamp
items.description: A tiny holographic stamp handed out by the event booth for catching limited-print troublemakers.

npcs.name: Marshal Echo
npcs.description: A volunteer marshal in a reflective vest, holding a clipboard like it can legally stop a dragon.

combat.defeatMessage: Marshal Echo blows a whistle and calls a timeout.

greeting: Welcome to Binder Square. If your deck starts glowing, point it away from traffic.

response: The mall turnstile needs a sleeve key. The card shop north kept the emergency one, because of course the card shop became important immediately.
Grab it, open the mall gate, and check on the kiosk kid inside before he starts issuing quests on receipt paper.
At 3:14 PM every card game in town decided rules were optional and monsters were mandatory.
Good news: most of them respond to confidence. Bad news: confidence is not a fire extinguisher.

npcs.name: Nina Sleeves
npcs.description: A card shop owner with a price gun, a first-aid kit, and the terrifying calm of retail experience.

combat.defeatMessage: Nina ducks behind a counter and declares you banned for six minutes.

greeting: Singles are half off if they are not currently alive. Please do not ask me to price the ceiling dragon.

response: Take the sleeve key if Marshal Echo sent you. Pay me back by keeping the mall from becoming a booster-pack volcano.
They are not evil. Mostly. They are like pets, fireworks, and terms-of-service updates all at once.

npcs.name: Rogue Topdeck
npcs.description: A caped card knight dramatically topdecks itself every few seconds and gasps each time.

combat.defeatMessage: The Rogue Topdeck spins into a sleeve and sulks in foil silence.

greeting: The Rogue Topdeck points at you, then at the sky, then at its own reflection.

card.flavor: A dramatic opener that loudly believes every draw was destiny.

npcs.name: Snack Mimic
npcs.description: A shiny wrapper with googly eyes and too many teeth for a snack.

combat.defeatMessage: The Snack Mimic unwraps into ordinary trash and one edible snack.

greeting: crinkle crinkle

card.flavor: A wrapper-card hybrid best handled before lunch gets ideas.

npcs.name: Signal DJ Pax
npcs.description: A teen DJ broadcasting monster alerts between jingles from behind a pretzel cart.

combat.defeatMessage: Pax drops the mic, catches it, and calls that a tactical retreat.

greeting: Binder Bay Radio is live, improvised, and only a little on fire.

response: The roof mast needs an antenna sticker and a signal booster. Get the sticker from transit, the booster from the roof, then tell the technician we can go citywide.
Every good emergency needs a theme song. I am taking requests after the flying vending machine lands.

npcs.name: Kiosk Kid Milo
npcs.description: A kid behind an information kiosk, armed with receipt paper, three markers, and terrifying logistics.

combat.defeatMessage: Milo ducks behind the kiosk and writes COMBAT IS NOT CUSTOMER SERVICE.

greeting: I have maps, coupons, and an evolving theory that the escalators are unionizing.

response: The mall is weird but navigable. Arcade east, lab through service, toy aisle west. If a sign says Final Sale, do not duel it.
Tell the marshal I am alive, helpful, and only charging one coupon per rescue.

npcs.name: Coupon Knight
npcs.description: A cardboard knight with a lance made of expired coupons.

combat.defeatMessage: The Coupon Knight yields and prints a raincheck.

greeting: The Coupon Knight declares, "Buy one peril, get one free!"

card.flavor: A discount champion with expired offers and valid confidence.

npcs.name: Pixel Drake
npcs.description: A blocky little dragon made of arcade pixels, tickets, and poor impulse control.

combat.defeatMessage: The Pixel Drake drops into attract mode and stops biting.

greeting: The Pixel Drake makes a coin-in sound with its mouth.

card.flavor: A coin-op dragon with excellent sound effects and no indoor voice.

npcs.name: Roof Technician Bea
npcs.description: A technician zip-tied to a safety harness and absolutely done with magical static.

combat.defeatMessage: Bea points to the safety sign until you feel bad.

greeting: If this mast explodes into confetti, I am invoicing the entire concept of card games.

response: Sticker plus booster equals citywide warning. Probably. If the warning comes out as a guitar solo, that is still useful.

npcs.name: Weather Wyrm
npcs.description: A serpentine forecast graphic escaped from a weather app and now predicts punches.

combat.defeatMessage: The Weather Wyrm collapses into a seven-day forecast for mild embarrassment.

greeting: The Weather Wyrm hisses: "Chance of chaos: one hundred percent."

card.flavor: A forecast serpent whose seven-day outlook is mostly trouble.

npcs.name: Professor Amper
npcs.description: A researcher in safety goggles, holding a prism shard with barbecue tongs.

combat.defeatMessage: Professor Amper retreats behind a trifold display.

greeting: Excellent, a test subject. I mean helper. I said helper.

response: The lab leak is sorting monsters by color and mood. Bring me a prism shard, then check the vault before the colors start voting.
Science is what we call it when screaming becomes repeatable.

npcs.name: Cardboard Golem
npcs.description: A hulking golem folded from bulk cards, packing tape, and very big feelings.

combat.defeatMessage: The Cardboard Golem collapses into a recyclable heap.

greeting: The Cardboard Golem creaks like a thousand unsorted commons.

card.flavor: Bulk cards, packing tape, and one extremely committed silhouette.

npcs.name: Mana Squall
npcs.description: A swirling spellstorm that keeps becoming five colors and one bad idea.

combat.defeatMessage: The Mana Squall sorts itself into a neat little badge.

greeting: The Mana Squall crackles in several incompatible rules systems.

card.flavor: Five colors, one storm, and zero patience for clean timing windows.

npcs.name: Ranger Mira
npcs.description: A park ranger with bite-proof gloves and a whistle labeled PLEASE WORK.

combat.defeatMessage: Ranger Mira blows the whistle and everyone takes five.

greeting: Approach slowly, speak kindly, and never assume cute means harmless.

response: Grab capture tags from the ranger table or arcade, tag the park sightings, and report back before the little ones discover the hot dog cart.

npcs.name: Pocket Lizard
npcs.description: A tiny bright-eyed monster sitting in a card sleeve like it is a sleeping bag.

combat.defeatMessage: The Pocket Lizard curls up, tagged and calm.

greeting: The Pocket Lizard squeaks at the exact pitch of a new sidequest.

card.flavor: Small enough to nap in a sleeve, brave enough to start a chase scene.

npcs.name: Practice Dummy Deluxe
npcs.description: A foam dummy with a screen that says HIT ME RESPONSIBLY.

combat.defeatMessage: The dummy gives you a B plus and falls over.

greeting: The dummy squeaks: "Learning objective detected."

card.flavor: A training prop with professional-grade squeaks and feedback.

npcs.name: Mechanic Joss
npcs.description: A mechanic surrounded by runners, sprues, and tiny parts sorted with terrifying precision.

combat.defeatMessage: Joss lectures you about tool safety until combat becomes impossible.

greeting: Do not touch the red launch button. The blue one is also bad. Actually, hands in pockets.

response: The frame bay door needs the servo key from the training lot. Get in, check the launch rail, and stop the plastic mecha before it discovers stairs.

npcs.name: Plastic Mecha
npcs.description: A towering snap-together battle frame, half assembled and fully confident.

combat.defeatMessage: The Plastic Mecha loses one dramatic shoulder piece and powers down.

greeting: The Plastic Mecha points at the sky even though the ceiling is right there.

card.flavor: A snap-frame titan powered by decals, drama, and loose polycaps.

npcs.name: Captain Rook
npcs.description: A waterfront volunteer wearing a captain hat over a bike helmet.

combat.defeatMessage: Captain Rook calls a snack break and refuses to duel volunteers.

greeting: Welcome aboard the pier, which is legally still a sidewalk.

response: Grab captain tokens from the harbor or pier. The rubber-band brute respects bold declarations and also snacks. Mostly bold declarations.
The pavilion is where everything crashes together. Bring a crossover pass unless you enjoy arguing with sentient badge scanners.

npcs.name: Rubber-Band Brute
npcs.description: A stretchy brawler made of rubber bands, pirate stickers, and unearned confidence.

combat.defeatMessage: The Rubber-Band Brute snaps back to normal size and looks embarrassed.

greeting: The Rubber-Band Brute winds up from approximately too far away.

card.flavor: A stretchy dockside bruiser that treats distance as a suggestion.

npcs.name: Coordinator Vale
npcs.description: An event coordinator with six lanyards, seven schedules, and no remaining fear.

combat.defeatMessage: Vale reschedules the fight for never.

greeting: If one more universe enters from stage left, I am billing overtime to destiny.

response: Take the crossover pass, get it through the stage gate, and stop the genre jam in the pavilion before every rulebook merges into soup.

npcs.name: Crossover Glitch
npcs.description: A flickering silhouette made of mismatched card frames and surprise guest stars.

combat.defeatMessage: The Crossover Glitch resolves into a harmless sticker sheet.

greeting: The Crossover Glitch speaks in three catchphrases at once.

card.flavor: A rules collision that arrives with three guest-star entrances.

npcs.name: Genre-Jam Titan
npcs.description: A huge monster made from duel capes, tiny companions, spell prisms, model parts, pirate banners, and crossover badges.

combat.defeatMessage: The Genre-Jam Titan splits into a dozen tired cards and one very relieved city.

greeting: The Genre-Jam Titan raises six opening themes at once.

card.flavor: The citywide finale compressed into one impossible card frame.

npcs.name: Holo Topdeck
npcs.description: A mirror-bright version of Rogue Topdeck that sparkles hard enough to interrupt traffic.

card.flavor: A limited shimmer print that insists every entrance deserves a spotlight.

combat.defeatMessage: The Holo Topdeck flashes one last dramatic glint and slides into a premium sleeve.

greeting: The Holo Topdeck reflects your heroic pose back at maximum brightness.

npcs.name: Prismatic Pocket Lizard
npcs.description: A pocket-sized companion monster refracting sunlight into tiny victory poses.

card.flavor: A cheerful event variant that turns every rescue tag into a parade ribbon.

combat.defeatMessage: The Prismatic Pocket Lizard chirps, accepts a tag, and sparkles into your binder log.

greeting: The Prismatic Pocket Lizard squeaks in limited-edition colors.

npcs.name: Cabinet Boss Drake
npcs.description: A pixel dragon with a boss health bar hovering where common sense should be.

card.flavor: A bonus-stage variant with flashing borders and exactly one continue left.

combat.defeatMessage: The Cabinet Boss Drake blinks GAME CLEAR and coughs up a burst of tickets.

greeting: The Cabinet Boss Drake roars in sixteen-bit surround sound.

npcs.name: Cross-Promo Mecha
npcs.description: A model-frame monster wearing too many sponsor decals and enjoying all of them.

card.flavor: A showcase collab print with enough decals to qualify as a parade float.

combat.defeatMessage: The Cross-Promo Mecha sheds three decals, salutes every franchise, and powers down.

greeting: The Cross-Promo Mecha announces itself in six logos at once.

npcs.name: Trapline Busker
npcs.description: A sidewalk performer juggling face-down cards and acting shocked when every one is a trap.

card.flavor: A street-corner trick card with a hat full of dramatic reveals.

combat.defeatMessage: The Trapline Busker bows, sweeps the trap cards into a neat pile, and vanishes behind applause nobody ordered.

greeting: The Trapline Busker asks you to pick a card, then picks the loudest one for you.

npcs.name: Plush Spark Buddy
npcs.description: A stuffed companion mascot crackling with static and extremely sincere courage.

card.flavor: A soft companion print with brave button eyes and a tiny thunder pose.

combat.defeatMessage: The Plush Spark Buddy gives one last determined squeak and settles into rescue mode.

greeting: The Plush Spark Buddy vibrates with the confidence of a toy aisle champion.

npcs.name: Dockside Deckhand
npcs.description: A cheerful dockside brawler made of rope loops, sticker scars, and bold declarations.

card.flavor: A dockside crew card that treats every railing like a dramatic entrance.

combat.defeatMessage: The Dockside Deckhand salutes, misses the pier post, and decides that still counts.

greeting: The Dockside Deckhand shouts that the crew has arrived, then looks around for the crew.

npcs.name: Token Mascot
npcs.description: A round arcade-prize mascot rolling on token feet and chanting a victory jingle.

card.flavor: A low-score arcade card that becomes priceless if you hum the jingle.

combat.defeatMessage: The Token Mascot rolls into the prize counter and awards itself a participation sticker.

greeting: The Token Mascot jingles like a prize machine with plot relevance.

npcs.name: Continuity Cameo
npcs.description: A cheerful guest-frame silhouette waving from the wrong side of the schedule.

card.flavor: A cameo card that looks familiar from exactly nowhere official.

combat.defeatMessage: The Continuity Cameo waves, exits through the wrong panel, and leaves the schedule slightly cleaner.

greeting: The Continuity Cameo introduces itself with a theme sting nobody recognizes.

npcs.name: Credits Colossus
npcs.description: A towering encore monster made of scrolling credits, recap cards, and one last impossible pose.

card.flavor: An encore card printed after the ending because the city still had confetti left.

combat.defeatMessage: The Credits Colossus scrolls past SPECIAL THANKS and finally sits down.

greeting: The Credits Colossus raises a glowing card that says AND ONE MORE THING.
